Non-portable ARM-specific implementation of setjmp and longjmp functions with jmp_buf structure.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides a non-portable implementation of [`setjmp`](<#setjmp>) and [`longjmp`](<#longjmp>) functions specifically for ARM architecture. It defines a structure `__jmp_buf_struct` to store the state of general-purpose registers (GPRs) and, if applicable, floating-point unit (FPU) registers when using ARM's vector floating-point (VFP) hardware. The `jmp_buf` type is defined as an array of this structure, which is used by the [`setjmp`](<#setjmp>) and [`longjmp`](<#longjmp>) functions to save and restore the execution context. If the code is not compiled on an ARM architecture, it includes the standard system `setjmp.h` header file instead. The file notes that this implementation is non-portable and will require changes if the architecture changes from ARM.
# Data Structures

---
### \_\_jmp\_buf\_struct
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `r4`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r4.
    - `r5`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r5.
    - `r6`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r6.
    - `r7`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r7.
    - `r8`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r8.
    - `r9`: Stores the value of the general-purpose register r9.
    - `sl`: Stores the value of the stack limit register.
    - `fp`: Stores the value of the frame pointer register.
    - `sp`: Stores the value of the stack pointer register.
    - `lr`: Stores the value of the link register.
    - `s`: Stores the values of the floating-point registers s16 to s31 when using a real FPU.
    - `fpscr`: Stores the value of the floating-point status and control register.
- **Description**: Defines a non-portable data structure for ARM architecture to store the state of CPU registers, including general-purpose and floating-point registers, for use with `setjmp` and `longjmp` functions.
